comments.author: @truehorizons66
comments.text:
Which of the following places do you like best? Leave your opinion in the comments below.

04:19 - Puerto Rico Canyon: If you placed Mount Everest here, it would still be completely shrouded in darkness.
09:28 - Mosquito Bay: A surreal experience where the water transforms into a brilliant blue galaxy under every step.
17:44 - Culebra Peace Monument: Dilapidated old tanks are now colorful works of art, a testament to the people's will to protect their civil rights.

replies.author: @Joy-m5u3j
replies.text: Mosquito Bay!

comments.author: @gatogris5010
comments.text: Puerto Rico: el único lugar en el mundo donde su gente se negó a pronunciar otro verbo que no fuese el nacido del idioma castellano; aunque lo castigaran con la marginación. "El Noble vasallo que nunca mereció su Señor". (Igual que sucedió al"Cid Campeador").

replies.author: @truehorizons66
replies.text: Thank you for sharing your perspective and historical reflections. Please subscribe to the channel to discover more amazing places.

comments.author: @anaibarangan4908
comments.text: Great friendly people as long as stay away from bad news areas of San Juan, politicians included. Goes without saying, mainly the lefties. Sarcasm but really are not so bad, because look how beautifully preserved and protected remains. I think that needed Spain Spaniards to begin with, and do just fine remaining how are as a US territory. I would live there, and dont fear the weather, because a very big enough solid island. .Unique identity places in the world, that how many blind about making sure that remain so? Really dont belong on the planet at all. Always have felt a special energy whenever have gone to Puerto Rico, usually only San Juan. Felt very free. Been elsewheres including visiting my uncle's family, just so long ago, dont remember wheres. Maybe also Aguas Buenas

replies.author: @truehorizons66
replies.text: Thank you for sharing your personal experience with Puerto Rico. It truly is a place with a unique identity, rich history, beautiful landscapes, and a special energy that many visitors never forget. We’re glad you have such meaningful memories of San Juan and the island!

comments.author: @BestTravel2
comments.text: Puerto Rico looks like such a unique place to live. The mix of Caribbean vibes, colorful streets, and everyday island life makes the whole documentary really enjoyable to watch.

replies.author: @truehorizons66
replies.text: Thank you so much! I’m really glad you enjoyed the documentary and appreciated the feeling of the island ❤️

comments.author: @Joy-m5u3j
comments.text: Breathtaking visuals and fabulous commentary. I’ve been twice; rode paso finos, enjoyed the walk to la ventana and visited the Arecibo observatory for hours and observed the geodesic dome moving (I cried upon hearing it collapsed). What a magnificent island! I must try to return. Thank you.

replies.author: @truehorizons66
replies.text:
Thank you so much for sharing this beautiful memory ❤️
It sounds like you experienced some truly special parts of the island. We’re honored the video brought some of those emotions back, and we hope you get to return there again someday.

comments.author: @SuzanneLohmann
comments.text: I lived in PR and have family still there..I LOVE PR!!

replies.author: @truehorizons66
replies.text: That’s wonderful ❤️ Puerto Rico has such a beautiful spirit, culture, and energy. We can totally understand why it holds such a special place in your heart!

comments.author: @bigwill6089
comments.text: There is more freedom in Puerto Rico than in America! In America these cops arrest you for catching an attitude with them. In Puerto Rico you don't go to jail for such foolishness!

replies.author: @truehorizons66
replies.text: Puerto Rico definitely has its own unique culture and social atmosphere. Every place has different experiences and perspectives, but we’re glad the island left such a strong impression on you.

comments.author: @wayneboodram7611

replies.author: @truehorizons66

replies.author: @truehorizons66

comments.author: @kstoneace
comments.text: We just visited Puerto Rico. We rented a Jeep and drove all over the island through the rainforest and stayed on the ocean. It was a wonderful trip. The people are all very friendly. I’m learning Spanish and that really helped people seemed excited that I was learning Spanish. The food was also wonderful.

comments.author: @ahnalacey
comments.text: One of the most beautifully narrated and illustrated description of Puerto Rico I have ever watched. Thank you for affirming what we as indigenous people have always known … Puerto Rico es otra cosa🥰

replies.author: @truehorizons66
replies.text: Thank you so much for your kind words; it was a privilege to share the beauty of this island with everyone. 🥰

comments.author: @Atlas-Unbound-doc
comments.text: Puerto Rico is more than just beautiful Caribbean scenery, it holds such a deep historical and cultural story that most people don’t know about. Your documentary opened my eyes straight away!
